keyPoints: NTP (Network Time Protocol) is used for synchronizing clocks over networks.
Clocks can be synchronized using wireless protocols like Wi-Fi Time Synchronization.
The system clock can be synchronized with the network time by periodically querying a time server.
The synchronization process involves adjusting the local clock based on the time received from the time server.
The frequency of synchronization depends on the system's requirements and the available network bandwidth.
